intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Good — I can see the GSM5746268 matrix triplet. Before running the full pipeline, let me check the recipe for scanpy single-cell QC + clustering to ensure I follow the best practices:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "description": "Standard scanpy processing for ONE scRNA-seq sample.", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + first-pass clustering (scanpy). Halt and present_plan before running on an unfamiliar dataset \u2014 the QC thresholds and the clustering resolution are dataset-dependent. Steps: sc.read_10x_mtx; calculate_qc_metrics; filter cells (n_genes>=200) & genes; filter pct_counts_mt; normalize_total(target_sum=1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; scale; pca; neighbors; leiden; umap; rank_genes_groups (wilcoxon) for markers. Plots: QC violin, HVG, UMAP colored by leiden, marker dotplot."}

blocks.text: Perfect — the recipe is clear. Before I proceed with the full workflow, let me load the data and do a quick QC inspection so we can set sensible thresholds together. I'll start with the data load and initial QC metrics:

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import os
import gzip
from scipy.io import mmread
from pathlib import Path

# Define the sample prefix
sample_prefix = 'GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1'

# Paths to the matrix triplet
matrix_path = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, f'{sample_prefix}.matrix.mtx.gz')
barcodes_path = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, f'{sample_prefix}.barcodes.tsv.gz')
features_path = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, f'{sample_prefix}.features.tsv.gz')

print(f"Loading 10x matrix triplet from {sample_prefix}...")

# Load using scanpy's 10x reader (works with gzipped files)
adata = sc.read_10x_mtx(
    os.path.dirname(matrix_path),
    prefix=sample_prefix + '.',
    first_category_is_gene_names=False
)

print(f"Loaded: {adata.shape[0]} cells × {adata.shape[1]} genes")
print(f"Matrix type: {adata.X.dtype}, sparsity: {1 - adata.X.nnz / (adata.shape[0] * adata.shape[1]):.2%}")

# Calculate QC metrics
sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata, inplace=True)

print("\n=== QC METRICS SUMMARY ===")
print(f"n_genes per cell: {adata.obs['n_genes'].describe()}")
print(f"\nn_counts per cell: {adata.obs['n_counts'].describe()}")
print(f"\npct_counts_mt: {adata.obs['pct_counts_mt'].describe()}")
print(f"\nGenes per cluster: {adata.var['n_cells'].describe()}")

# Show distribution visually
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(12, 3))
axes[0].hist(adata.obs['n_genes'], bins=50, color='steelblue', edgecolor='black')
axes[0].set_xlabel('n_genes')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Count')
axes[1].hist(adata.obs['n_counts'], bins=50, color='steelblue', edgecolor='black')
axes[1].set_xlabel('n_counts')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Count')
axes[2].hist(adata.obs['pct_counts_mt'], bins=50, color='steelblue', edgecolor='black')
axes[2].set_xlabel('pct_counts_mt')
axes[2].set_ylabel('Count')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('qc_metrics_initial.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()

print("\nQC metrics plot saved.")
